LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS ANN CHAN, JUDY TO AND SIMON CHAN'S MOTION TO TRANSFER ACTION TO SAN DIEGO AND FOR SANCTIONS. A R G U E D; THE COURT VACATED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AND RULED AS FOLLOWS: GRANTED WITHOUT PREJUDICE FOR PLAINTIFF TO REFILE AS A NEW CASE AT THE SAN FRANCISCO PROBATE COURT UNDER THE PROBATE CODE SECTION 7051 OR 17005. HOWEVER, IF THE PLAINTIFF IS INCORRECT, SANCTION TO BE AWARDED IN FAVOR OF THE DEFENDANTS. SANCTION IS AWARDED IN THE AMOUNT OF $1,500.OO FOR THE DEFENDANTS. MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES - ATTY. CARRIE MOUTTON OF THE HALLISEY & JOHNSON, P.C., TEL. NO. 433-5300 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. HENRY TO, APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANTS AND MOVING PARTIES.

RULING - A R G U E D; THE COURT VACATED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AND RULED AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANTS ANN CHAN, JUDY TO AND SIMON CHAN'S Motion To Transfer Action to San Diego And For Sanctions is granted without prejudice for Plaintiff to refile as a new case at the San Francisco Probate Court under the Probate Code Section 7051 or 17005. However, if the Plaintiff is incorrect, sanction to be awarded in favor of the defendants. SANCTION IS AWARDED IN THE AMOUNT OF $1,500.OO FOR THE DEFENDANTS. PARTIES TO MEET AND CONFER AS TO FORM OF ORDER. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR # 7770 =302/HEK
